prompt: Produce a 4-step assessment rubric for the following learning outcome: 'Students will be able to write a well-structured argumentative essay.'
reference: Step 1: Clear thesis and position. Step 2: Logical paragraph structure with topic sentences. Step 3: Evidence and citation to support claims. Step 4: Coherent conclusion that revisits thesis.

prompt: Outline a 5-step plan for a student preparing for a closed-book exam in 1 week.
reference: 1. Review syllabus and past papers. 2. Identify weak areas. 3. Active recall using flashcards or practice tests. 4. Timed mock exam under exam conditions. 5. Rest and light review the day before.

prompt: Design a 4-step process for peer feedback on a written report.
reference: 1. Read the full report for overall impression. 2. Comment on structure and argument clarity. 3. Evaluate evidence and citations. 4. Suggest specific improvements and highlight strengths.

prompt: Create a 5-step onboarding plan for a new postgraduate research student joining a lab.
reference: 1. Introduce lab policies and safety procedures. 2. Set up accounts and system access. 3. Meeting with supervisor to set 3-month goals. 4. Literature review on project topic. 5. Attend first lab meeting and present initial findings.

prompt: Outline a 4-step rubric for evaluating an oral group presentation.
reference: 1. Content accuracy and depth. 2. Organisation and logical flow. 3. Delivery: clarity, pace, eye contact. 4. Teamwork: equal contribution and smooth handovers.

prompt: Describe a 5-step workflow for conducting a literature review for a research project.
reference: 1. Define research question and keywords. 2. Search databases (e.g., Google Scholar, Scopus). 3. Screen titles and abstracts for relevance. 4. Read and synthesise selected papers. 5. Organise findings into themes and identify gaps.

prompt: Propose a 4-step plan for a student who has missed two weeks of lectures due to illness and needs to catch up.
reference: 1. Contact lecturer and obtain missed notes/recordings. 2. Identify key concepts and assess understanding. 3. Complete missed tasks and seek clarification if needed. 4. Develop a revised study schedule to stay current.

prompt: Design a 5-step protocol for setting up a new collaborative student project team.
reference: 1. Establish roles and responsibilities. 2. Agree on communication channels and meeting schedule. 3. Define project goals and timeline. 4. Set up shared document workspace. 5. Create a conflict resolution process.

prompt: Create a 4-step rubric for assessing a data analysis assignment.
reference: 1. Correct data cleaning and handling. 2. Appropriate statistical methods applied. 3. Clear visualisations with labels and interpretation. 4. Discussion of limitations and implications.

prompt: Outline a 5-step plan for designing and running a classroom survey to evaluate teaching effectiveness.
reference: 1. Define objectives and target questions. 2. Design the survey instrument (rating + open). 3. Pilot test with a small group. 4. Administer survey and collect responses. 5. Analyse results and report to stakeholders.

prompt: Write a 4-step process for transitioning a face-to-face course to online delivery.
reference: 1. Audit existing content for suitability online. 2. Record and caption video lectures. 3. Set up LMS with activities, discussion boards, and assessments. 4. Communicate changes and provide student orientation.

prompt: Propose a 5-step mentoring programme for pairing senior and junior students.
reference: 1. Recruit and brief mentors and mentees. 2. Match pairs based on study area and goals. 3. Facilitate introductory meeting with structured agenda. 4. Schedule regular check-ins (fortnightly). 5. Evaluate programme at semester end.

prompt: Outline a 4-step process for a student writing a technical lab report.
reference: 1. Record all experimental observations and data during the lab. 2. Draft introduction and method sections from notes. 3. Analyse results and create figures/tables. 4. Write discussion and conclusion, then proofread.

prompt: Design a 5-step plan for organising an academic conference panel session.
reference: 1. Identify panel topic and invite 4-5 speakers. 2. Circulate abstract template and collect submissions. 3. Assign time slots and prepare chair questions. 4. Conduct tech rehearsal. 5. Facilitate panel with Q&A and wrap up.

prompt: Create a 4-step rubric for assessing student participation in class discussions.
reference: 1. Frequency and consistency of contributions. 2. Quality of arguments and use of evidence. 3. Respectful engagement with peers' views. 4. Ability to build on and synthesise discussion points.

prompt: Write a 5-step workflow for a research team managing shared data ethically.
reference: 1. Define data ownership and access rights. 2. Anonymise data before analysis. 3. Store data on secure institutional servers. 4. Obtain ethics approval for data collection. 5. Plan for data retention and disposal per policy.

prompt: Outline a 4-step plan for preparing a student for their first job interview.
reference: 1. Research the company and role requirements. 2. Prepare answers to common questions using STAR method. 3. Practice with a mock interview. 4. Prepare questions to ask the interviewer and review logistics.

prompt: Design a 5-step process for developing a new elective course for a university programme.
reference: 1. Identify demand through student and industry consultation. 2. Draft learning outcomes and assessment strategy. 3. Develop content and select resources. 4. Seek departmental and academic board approval. 5. Pilot with a small cohort and revise.

prompt: Propose a 4-step academic integrity awareness campaign for first-year students.
reference: 1. Define academic integrity and common violations. 2. Deliver workshop with case studies. 3. Run plagiarism-checker training session. 4. Distribute resources and open Q&A channel.

prompt: Write a 5-step plan for a student managing multiple assignment deadlines in the same week.
reference: 1. List all tasks and their deadlines. 2. Estimate time required per task. 3. Prioritise by deadline and weight. 4. Block dedicated work times in the calendar. 5. Build in review time before each submission.

prompt: Outline a 4-step process for building a student study group.
reference: 1. Recruit members with similar goals and availability. 2. Agree on ground rules, roles, and schedule. 3. Divide topics for each member to teach the group. 4. Review progress and adjust approach after each session.

prompt: Create a 5-step research data collection plan for a social science survey study.
reference: 1. Define research questions and hypotheses. 2. Design and pilot questionnaire. 3. Obtain ethics clearance. 4. Recruit participants and collect data. 5. Clean and code data ready for analysis.

prompt: Design a 4-step rubric for a capstone project proposal.
reference: 1. Clarity and feasibility of the research question. 2. Relevance to literature and identified gap. 3. Proposed methodology and timeline. 4. Quality of writing and references.

prompt: Write a 5-step plan for introducing a new learning management system to academic staff.
reference: 1. Conduct needs assessment and select LMS. 2. Pilot with volunteer early adopters. 3. Develop training materials and run workshops. 4. Migrate existing content from old system. 5. Provide ongoing helpdesk support and gather feedback.

prompt: Propose a 4-step conflict resolution process for student project teams.
reference: 1. Identify the issue clearly from each party's perspective. 2. Facilitate structured discussion with agreed ground rules. 3. Agree on a solution and document it. 4. Monitor and follow up to ensure resolution holds.

prompt: Outline a 5-step process for a student applying for a competitive research scholarship.
reference: 1. Review eligibility criteria and gather required documents. 2. Draft personal statement and research proposal. 3. Request references from supervisors. 4. Proofread and tailor application to funder priorities. 5. Submit before the deadline and acknowledge receipt.

prompt: Design a 4-step plan for reviewing and updating an outdated course curriculum.
reference: 1. Benchmark against current industry and academic standards. 2. Gather feedback from graduates and employers. 3. Revise learning outcomes, content, and assessments. 4. Seek approval and communicate changes to students.

prompt: Write a 5-step workflow for an academic publishing a journal article.
reference: 1. Draft manuscript and ensure it meets journal scope and guidelines. 2. Get co-author and colleague feedback. 3. Submit and handle peer-review revisions. 4. Proofread accepted manuscript and approve proofs. 5. Promote publication through academic networks.

prompt: Create a 4-step rubric for evaluating the quality of an annotated bibliography.
reference: 1. Accuracy and completeness of citation format. 2. Summary accurately reflects source content. 3. Critical evaluation of source's strengths and limitations. 4. Relevance to the stated research focus.

prompt: Outline a 5-step plan for a tutor giving constructive written feedback on student essays.
reference: 1. Read the full essay before writing any comments. 2. Identify 2-3 main strengths. 3. Identify 2-3 priority areas for improvement with specific examples. 4. Suggest actionable next steps. 5. Write a brief overall summary at the end.

prompt: Design a 4-step process for a teaching team co-developing a shared assessment.
reference: 1. Agree on learning outcomes being assessed. 2. Draft assessment task and rubric collaboratively. 3. Moderate sample responses to ensure consistent marking. 4. Review results and refine rubric for future cohorts.

prompt: Write a 5-step plan for inducting a new member of academic support staff.
reference: 1. Welcome meeting with team and manager. 2. System access setup and policy overview. 3. Shadowing experienced colleague for first week. 4. Assign initial tasks with supervision. 5. Two-week check-in and 3-month review.

prompt: Propose a 4-step rubric for assessing a student's reflective learning journal.
reference: 1. Depth of reflection on experience. 2. Evidence of critical thinking and self-assessment. 3. Connection to theory and course content. 4. Clarity and coherence of writing.

prompt: Outline a 5-step process for converting assessment feedback into a student action plan.
reference: 1. Read feedback carefully and highlight recurring themes. 2. Categorise as content, structure, or skills issues. 3. Set 3 specific improvement goals. 4. Identify resources or support needed. 5. Review progress at next submission.

prompt: Create a 4-step workflow for a student conducting an informational interview with a professional.
reference: 1. Research the person's background and company. 2. Prepare 5-7 targeted questions. 3. Conduct the interview and take notes. 4. Send a thank-you message and reflect on key takeaways.

prompt: Design a 5-step accessibility audit for an online course.
reference: 1. Check all videos have accurate captions. 2. Ensure all images have descriptive alt text. 3. Verify documents are screen-reader compatible. 4. Test navigation with keyboard-only mode. 5. Gather feedback from students with accessibility needs.

prompt: Write a 4-step plan for a student preparing a 10-minute conference presentation.
reference: 1. Structure content: introduction, 2-3 key points, conclusion. 2. Create clear slides with minimal text. 3. Practise aloud at least three times, timing each run. 4. Prepare for Q&A with likely questions.

prompt: Outline a 5-step process for revising a failed assignment and resubmitting.
reference: 1. Read all feedback carefully and identify root causes of failure. 2. Consult lecturer or tutor for clarification. 3. Revise the assignment addressing each feedback point. 4. Ask a peer to review the revised version. 5. Submit with a cover note detailing changes made.

prompt: Propose a 4-step framework for a student setting effective academic goals at the start of a semester.
reference: 1. Review past performance and identify gaps. 2. Set SMART goals for grades and skills. 3. Break goals into monthly milestones. 4. Schedule regular review points to track progress.

prompt: Design a 5-step process for a student requesting a disability-related academic accommodation.
reference: 1. Register with the disability support office. 2. Provide required medical documentation. 3. Meet with support advisor to discuss needs. 4. Accommodation letter issued to relevant lecturers. 5. Review arrangements at start of each semester.

prompt: Create a 4-step rubric for assessing a student's case study analysis.
reference: 1. Accurate identification of the core problem. 2. Application of relevant theory or frameworks. 3. Evidence-based recommendations. 4. Logical structure and professional presentation.

prompt: Write a 5-step plan for building a portfolio of work-ready evidence during an undergraduate degree.
reference: 1. Identify key competencies employers seek. 2. Collect artefacts (projects, reports, awards) from each semester. 3. Write brief reflections on each item. 4. Curate in a digital or physical portfolio format. 5. Update and tailor for each job or internship application.

prompt: Outline a 4-step plan for running an effective online tutorial session.
reference: 1. Send agenda and materials 24 hours before. 2. Begin with a brief recap and check-in. 3. Facilitate discussion or problem-solving activity. 4. End with a summary and clear next steps.

prompt: Design a 5-step process for an instructor implementing active learning in a large lecture.
reference: 1. Identify 2-3 points in the lecture to pause for activity. 2. Design brief tasks: polls, think-pair-share, or problem-solving. 3. Use classroom technology to gather responses. 4. Debrief responses and correct misconceptions. 5. Adjust future lecture design based on response patterns.

prompt: Write a 4-step rubric for marking a student's research poster.
reference: 1. Scientific accuracy and clarity of main message. 2. Logical layout and visual design. 3. Use of data and figures. 4. Student's ability to explain and answer questions.

prompt: Propose a 5-step plan for a student navigating a grade dispute.
reference: 1. Review the marking rubric and feedback thoroughly. 2. Identify specific criteria where you believe the mark is incorrect. 3. Request an informal meeting with the marker. 4. If unresolved, submit a formal regrade request with written justification. 5. Follow institutional appeals procedure if still unsatisfied.

prompt: Design a 4-step process for developing a student wellness programme.
reference: 1. Survey students to identify key wellbeing needs. 2. Partner with counselling, health, and sport services. 3. Design and pilot programme activities. 4. Evaluate impact through follow-up survey and participation data.

prompt: Outline a 5-step workflow for evaluating the impact of a teaching innovation.
reference: 1. Define success metrics before implementation. 2. Collect baseline data from a comparison cohort or prior year. 3. Implement innovation and collect participant data. 4. Analyse data against baseline. 5. Write report and share findings with teaching team.

prompt: Create a 4-step plan for a student building professional connections during their degree.
reference: 1. Attend career fairs and professional events. 2. Join relevant student clubs or associations. 3. Connect with alumni via LinkedIn or institutional networks. 4. Conduct informational interviews with professionals of interest.

prompt: Write a 5-step process for a student completing a capstone project from proposal to submission.
reference: 1. Write and get proposal approved. 2. Conduct literature review and refine scope. 3. Collect and analyse data or develop prototype. 4. Write up findings in the required format. 5. Review with supervisor, incorporate feedback, and submit.
